---
title: Data-Driven Transport Planning Hackathon
date: 2026-05-18 14:00:00+01:00
author:
  - "Robin Lovelace"
---


Welcome to the **Data-Driven Transport Planning Hackathon**!

**Date:** Monday 18th May 2026  
**Time:** 14:00 – 17:00  
**Location:** David Keir Building, Room 0G.533

Hosted by **Robin Lovelace**, Professor of Transport Data Science at the University of Leeds and part of the team behind the **Propensity to Cycle Tool for Northern Ireland (PCTNI)**, this afternoon session is an open-ended challenge to develop data analysis, visualisation, or prototype tool ideas that help make transport planning more data-driven.

Refreshments will be provided. **Just bring yourself and your laptop!**

## The Challenge

Develop a tool, visualization, or analysis that addresses a transport planning problem.
While we provide datasets from the PCTNI project, your hackathon project does **not** have to be focused on cycling.

**Ideas for inspiration:**

- High-resolution accessibility mapping.
- Visualizing multimodal origin-destination flows.
- Predicting the impact of new transport infrastructure.
- Identifying gaps in existing data.

## External Datasets

Some suggestions for datasets:

Translink Bus Stops - [Translink Bus Stop List - Dataset - Open Data NI](https://admin.opendatani.gov.uk/dataset/translink-bus-stop-list)

Potholes (up to 2025) - [Pothole Enquiries - Dataset - Open Data NI](https://admin.opendatani.gov.uk/dataset/pothole-enquiries)

Railway Stations (population with a walk or drive distance)  - [Northern Ireland Railways Stations - Dataset - Open Data NI](https://admin.opendatani.gov.uk/dataset/northern-ireland-railways-stations)

Traffic Count Data (up to 2023 but later is available)  [Northern Ireland Traffic Count Data - Dataset - Open Data NI](https://admin.opendatani.gov.uk/dataset/northern-ireland-traffic-count-data)

Road Traffic Collision Statistics – collision data can be linked to vehicle and casualty data (available 2013-2024)  [Police Recorded Injury Road Traffic Collision Statistics Northern Ireland 2024 - Dataset - Open Data NI](https://admin.opendatani.gov.uk/dataset/police-recorded-injury-road-traffic-collision-statistics-northern-ireland-2024)

[Census 2021 origin-destination place of work tables (UK) | Northern Ireland Statistics and Research Agency](https://www.nisra.gov.uk/publications/census-2021-origin-destination-place-work-tables-uk) – for OD flows from 2021 census for NI – available at Census Data Zone.

## PCTNI Hackathon Datasets

These datasets are provided as part of the hackathon and can be downloaded from the [v0.1.0 Release](https://github.com/pctni/hack/releases/tag/v0.1.0).

### Geography and Demand

- **[Zones (SDZ 2021)](https://github.com/pctni/hack/releases/download/v0.1.0/zones_sdz.gpkg)**: 2021 Super Data Zones for Northern Ireland (`zones_sdz.gpkg`).
- **[Origin-Destination (OD) Data](https://github.com/pctni/hack/releases/download/v0.1.0/od_ni_open_filtered.csv)**: Filtered 2021 Census OD data for workplace destinations (`od_ni_open_filtered.csv`).

### Route Networks

- **[Fastest Route Network](https://github.com/pctni/hack/releases/download/v0.1.0/route_network_fastest.gpkg)**: Network representing the fastest routes (`route_network_fastest.gpkg`).
- **[Quietest Route Network](https://github.com/pctni/hack/releases/download/v0.1.0/route_network_quietest.gpkg)**: Network representing the quietest routes (`route_network_quietest.gpkg`).
- **[Processed Cycle Network](https://github.com/pctni/hack/releases/download/v0.1.0/cycle_net_processed.gpkg)**: A cleaned and processed version of the cycle network (`cycle_net_processed.gpkg`).

### High-Resolution Segments

The following files contain high-resolution route segments, available in both GeoPackage (`.gpkg`) and R Data (`.Rds`) formats:
- **Fastest Segments**: [Aug 2025](https://github.com/pctni/hack/releases/download/v0.1.0/segments_fastest_2025-08.gpkg), [18 Aug 2025](https://github.com/pctni/hack/releases/download/v0.1.0/segments_fastest_2025-08-18.gpkg), [19 Aug 2025](https://github.com/pctni/hack/releases/download/v0.1.0/segments_fastest_2025-08-19.gpkg).
- **Quietest Segments**: [Aug 2025](https://github.com/pctni/hack/releases/download/v0.1.0/segments_quietest_2025-08.gpkg), [18 Aug 2025](https://github.com/pctni/hack/releases/download/v0.1.0/segments_quietest_2025-08-18.gpkg), [19 Aug 2025](https://github.com/pctni/hack/releases/download/v0.1.0/segments_quietest_2025-08-19.gpkg).

## Registration

If you haven't already, please sign up for the event using the following form:
**[Hackathon Sign-up Form](https://docs.google.com/forms/d/1CfpigdYH3gLYhoaKraBhDYpmKS1PKefxarIFjp9loME/viewform?edit_requested=true)**

## Pre-event homework

To get a head start, we recommend that you try **importing and visualising** the [Core Hackathon Datasets](#core-hackathon-datasets) before the event. This will help you familiarise yourself with the data structure and ensure your technical setup is working correctly.

## Participation

-   Work individually or in teams.
-   Use any tools or languages (R, Python, JavaScript, etc.).
-   Focus on reproducibility and clear communication.

## Agenda (Monday 18th May)

| Time          | Activity                                    |
|---------------|---------------------------------------------|
| 14:00 - 14:45 | Getting started with the data and challenge |
| 14:45 - 15:00 | Break                                       |
| 15:00 - 16:30 | **Hackathon Session**                       |
| 16:30 - 17:00 | Presentation of results                     |

See the [Prerequisites](prerequisites.qmd) page to get ready!
